Japanese: １月　８日　午前　９時４６分 地方裁判所　被告人第１控え室
English: January 8, 9:46 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 1

Japanese: おはよーございますッ！ 成歩堂さん！
English: Gooood morning, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: おはよう、マコちゃん！
English: Good morning, Maggey!

Japanese: あの‥‥今日は、どうッスか？
English: So, what do you think is going to happen today, sir?

Japanese: きのうの法廷、ナゾだらけで 終わっちゃったッスけど‥‥
English: Yesterday's session didn't go so well, and then ended on a giant mystery.

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥）
English: (That's true...)

Japanese: （その“ナゾ”に関しては、 なんにもわかってないな‥‥）
English: (And we still haven't solved a single part of it yet...)

Japanese: だいじょうぶだよね、 なるほどくん？
English: Are you OK, Nick?

Japanese: え？　‥‥あー、うん。 もちろんだよ。
English: Huh? Oh, uh... Yeah. Of course.

Japanese: あ！　今ちょっとだけ、 アサッテの方を見たッス！
English: I saw that! That little flash of doubt in your eyes!

Japanese: ちち、ちがうよ！ ‥‥明るい明日を見すえただけだ。
English: N-No! That wasn't doubt. That was, umm... determination!

Japanese: うううう‥‥
English: Why don't I believe you...?

Japanese: そろそろ時間だよ、マコちゃん。 被告席に行かないと。
English: It's nearly time, Maggey. You'd better get going to the defendant's seat.

Japanese: じゃあ、よろしくおねがい しますね。成歩堂さん！
English: Roger. Don't let me down, Mr. Wright. I'm counting on you!

Japanese: コラァッ！　アンタ！
English: Hey, pal!

Japanese: あ‥‥イトノコさん‥‥
English: Hey, Detective Gumshoe...

Japanese: ダメじゃないッスか！ マコクンを不安にさせちゃあ！
English: Quit stressing Maggey out! She doesn't need that!

Japanese: なな、なんで 知ってるんですか？
English: H-How did you know she was stressed...?

Japanese: ドアのカゲから、 そっと見ていたッス。
English: I was watching through the doorway.
Oh...

Japanese: そんな、なさけないカオしちゃ ダメッス！
English: You look like you lost the case already. Show a bit of confidence, will you, pal!?

Japanese: そんなアンタには、これッス！
English: Here, maybe this'll help.

Japanese: イトノコさんも始めたんですか？ ‥‥アロマテラピー。
English: Huh!? Have you taken up aromatherapy, too?

Japanese: いやいや！　そうじゃないッス！ ‥‥おぼえてないッスか？　コレ。
English: Not in a million years, pal! Don't tell me that you don't remember this thing?

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥アロマテラピーの ボトルじゃないな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... Come to think of it, that doesn't look like one of those aromatherapy bottles...)

Japanese: ２日前、アンタが吐麗美庵の キッチンで見つけた小ビンッス！
English: This is the small bottle that turned up in Trés Bien's kitchen a couple of days ago.

Japanese: うわ、すごい！ 小さなビンがギッシリ。
English: Wow! Look at all these little bottles!

Japanese: アロマテラピーのオイルか。 床の上にまで、あふれてるぞ‥‥
English: Oh! They're aromatherapy oils! He's got so many, they're overflowing onto the floor...

Japanese: ‥‥あれ？
English: Hey, wait a minute...

Japanese: ‥‥このビンだけ、 なんかちがうよ。
English: There's one bottle that's different from all the others.

Japanese: そうだね‥‥。 ラベルもないし。
English: Well what do you know. And it doesn't have a label either.

Japanese: それに‥‥ニオイもしないよ。
English: And... *sniff* It doesn't smell.

Japanese: このビンの中身の分析結果が やっと出たッス！
English: We finally got the analysis results back from the lab.

Japanese: あ！　もしかして、あれですか！ モードク‥‥！
English: So!? What is it? Is it the poison?

Japanese: ザンネンながら、 ゼンゼンちがうッス。
English: I'm afraid not, pal.

Japanese: コレは‥‥クスリだったッス。
English: It's... medication.

Japanese: くすり‥‥？
English: Medication?

Japanese: 耳にたらして使う、外用薬ッス。
English: Yeah. For ears. Topical use only, apparently.

Japanese: “耳に”‥‥ってコトは、 もしかして‥‥？
English: For ears? You mean...?

Japanese: コマクが破れた被害者・岡　高夫に 処方されたクスリッス！
English: Yeah. It's the medication Glen Elg was using for his ruptured eardrum!

Japanese: （岡　高夫のクスリが‥‥ キッチンで発見された‥‥？）
English: (What was Glen Elg's ear medicine doing in the kitchen...?)

Japanese: 証拠品《小ビン》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Small Bottle refiled into the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥あの。イトノコ刑事？ 《正体不明の指紋》というのは‥‥
English: Um, what about the unidentified fingerprints? Anything on that?

Japanese: 鑑識の手ちがいで、ビンの中身の ブンセキしかしてなかったッス。
English: Someone screwed up, so they only had time to analyze the contents of the bottle.

Japanese: あと１時間もあれば、 指紋の検査もできたッスが‥‥
English: Another hour and they might have gotten something on the prints, but...

Japanese: （ううん‥‥証拠としては、 ヨワい気もするな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... That's going to weaken its impact as a piece of evidence...)

Japanese: さ。さ。時間ッス！ 今日こそ、キメるッスよ！
English: OK, pal! This is it! Make sure your defense is impregnable today, got it!?

Japanese: （‥‥今日の法廷‥‥）
English: (Today's trial...)

Japanese: （かならず、あのオトコを 引きずり出すんだ‥‥）
English: (I'm gonna expose that guy for what he's done, or my name isn't Phoenix Wright!)

Japanese: 同日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第４法廷
English: January 8, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 4

Japanese: これより、須々木　マコの 法廷を開廷します。
English: Court is now in session for the trial of Maggey Byrde.

Japanese: 弁護側、準備完了しております。
English: The defense is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: こっちは‥‥ 閉廷の準備ができてるぜ。
English: Ready and waiting as always. Your Honor.

Japanese: 毎度、たのもしいかぎりです。
English: Very good. Then we'll get underway at once.

Japanese: きのうの証人、五十嵐　将兵さんは こう証言しています。
English: Yesterday we heard the testimony of Mr. Victor Kudo.

Japanese: 被告人・須々木　マコが、被害者の コーヒーに粉末を入れた‥‥と。
English: He claims to have witnessed the defendant putting a powder into the victim's coffee.

Japanese: ただ‥‥五十嵐さんの証言には、 いくつかモンダイがありました。
English: However, the witness's testimony was plagued with a number of problems.

Japanese: カップの跡は、被害者が右手で コーヒーを飲んだことを示してる。
English: The mark on the rim of the cup shows that the victim drank from it with his right hand.

Japanese: しかし‥‥証言では、被害者は 左手でコーヒーを飲んでいる‥‥
English: But according to the old man's testimony, he picked it up with his left hand.

Japanese: さらに、五十嵐さんの 目撃証言によれば‥‥、
English: Thank you, Mr. Godot. Furthermore, according to the witness's account...

Japanese: 被害者は、聞こえるはずのない ラジオを聞いていました。
English: ...the victim was listening to the radio with an earpiece in his left ear.

Japanese: コマクが破れた左耳に、 イヤホンをつけて‥‥。
English: Yet, the victim's left eardrum was ruptured, which made him effectively deaf in that ear.

Japanese: いやー、ホント。ムジュン だらけだねえ、こりゃ。
English: It's amazing how many contradictions a single case can have, huh Nick?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　いいコトを 教えてやるぜ、裁判長‥‥。
English: Ha...! Allow me to enlighten you, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥地球は回り、オレたちも 回ってるんだぜ‥‥。
English: The world, you see, keeps turning, and we must turn with it.

Japanese: 今日もいきなり、 よくわからないのですが‥‥
English: You've lost me already, Mr. Godot.

Japanese: “きのうのナゾは、 今日もナゾ”‥‥
English: Don't let the mysteries of yesterday mystify you today.

Japanese: そんな考えかたは、もう お呼びじゃねえ、ってコトさ。
English: Only losers think like that.

Japanese: 時代は、変わるッ！ ‥‥そいつが、オレのルールだぜ。
English: You've got to change with the times! That's one of my rules!

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥あなたは！ 見つけたというのですか！
English: Are you implying that you've resolved these contradictions?

Japanese: このムジュンの‥‥コタエを！
English: You know the answers to these riddles!?

Japanese: マメを食う老人は、マメとともに 《真実》を投げていたのさ。
English: The old guy wasn't just throwing seed in here. He was throwing us off the scent.

Japanese: ‥‥そいつを、証明するぜ。
English: And today I'll prove it.

Japanese: それでは、最初の 証人を入廷させてください！
English: Very well. Let the first witness take the stand!

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ？　アンタ。
English: And you are?

Japanese: ボンジュウル、みなさあん。
English: Oh bonjour, everyone!

Japanese: 本土坊　カ・オ・ル。 シェフ、やってまあす。
English: I am Jean Armstrong, z'e owner and 'ead chef of la Trés Bien restaurant. Enchanté!

Japanese: ‥‥あの、シツレイですが、 証人はその‥‥女性、なのですか？
English: Forgive me for asking, witness, but... are you a woman?

Japanese: オー・ラ・ラ‥‥ ワタシ、花も恥じらうオ・ト・コ。
English: Oh là là, monsieur! As you can see, I am la pert and perky gentleman, non?

Japanese: ぐ‥‥ふううう‥‥。
English: Er... Umm...

Japanese: アンタ‥‥事件当時、吐麗美庵の キッチンにいた。
English: On the day of the incident, you were in Trés Bien's kitchen.

Japanese: ‥‥マチガイ、ねえな？
English: Isn't that right?

Japanese: ‥‥あなたとなら‥‥
English: W'iz you monsieur...

Japanese: マチガイを起こしちゃうかも‥‥
English: ...everything feels right!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
（すげェ！ まったく動じないぞ‥‥）
English: (Wow, he's totally unfazed! Doesn't anything intimidate this guy...?)

Japanese: それでは、さっそく 証言をしていただきましょう。
English: Very well. Your testimony please, witness.

Japanese: 事件が起こったとき‥‥ 吐麗美庵で、何があったか？
English: Please tell the court what happened that day at Trés Bien.

Japanese: ウイ、ヴォロンティエ！
English: Oui! Volontiers!

Japanese: ～吐麗美庵で何があったか？～
English: -- At Trés Bien --

Japanese: 事件があったとき、お客さんは ２人っきり、だったわねえ。
English: When it all 'appened, z'ere were just two customers in my restaurant.

Japanese: あの日‥‥実はウチ、インテリアの 改装工事をしてたのよォ。
English: I remember I was experimenting w'iz some new art deco z'at day.

Japanese: テーブルのあいだに、大きな カガミが置かれたりしたわぁ。
English: Like 'aving a large mirror between la tables, for example.

Japanese: あのオジさん、それを 見ちゃったんじゃないかしらねェ。
English: Oui! Per'aps z'at is what la old man was looking at.

Japanese: カップの手に、イヤホンにメガネ。 ゼンブ、左右が逆だったんだから。
English: La cup, la earpiece and la glasses... 'E would 'ave seen everyz'ing in reverse, non?

Japanese: カガミですってェェェッ！
English: MIRROR!?

Japanese: ウイ。グラン・ミロワール。 それは大きなカガミが、ねえ。
English: Oui! Un grand mirror! La most enormous mirror!

Japanese: ‥‥これで、 立証できたワケ、だぜ。
English: And suddenly, the mystery disappears!

Japanese: ‥‥地球は回り、オレたちも 回っている‥‥ってコトが、な。
English: Like I said, the world keeps turning, so roll with it!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 コーヒーカップに、イヤホン‥‥
English: Hmm... That would explain the coffee cup and the earpiece conundrum...

Japanese: たしかに、右と左が“逆”である ものばかりですなあ。
English: The mirror would have made everything appear back to front.

Japanese: （そんな‥‥バカな！）
English: (What the heck!? It's way too early in the morning for this to be happening to me!)

Japanese: それでは、弁護人！ 尋問をおねがいします！
English: Now then, Mr. Wright. You may begin your cross-examination.

Japanese: ～吐麗美庵で何があったか？～
English: -- At Trés Bien --

Japanese: 事件があったとき、お客さんは ２人っきり、だったわねえ。
English: When it all 'appened, z'ere were just two customers in my restaurant.

Japanese: あの日‥‥実はウチ、インテリアの 改装工事をしてたのよォ。
English: I remember I was experimenting w'iz some new art deco z'at day.

Japanese: テーブルのあいだに、大きな カガミが置かれたりしたわぁ。
English: Like 'aving a large mirror between la tables, for example.

Japanese: あのオジさん、それを 見ちゃったんじゃないかしらねェ。
English: Oui! Per'aps z'at is what la old man was looking at.

Japanese: カップの手に、イヤホンにメガネ。 ゼンブ、左右が逆だったんだから。
English: La cup, la earpiece and la glasses... 'E would 'ave seen everyz'ing in reverse, non?

Japanese: （‥‥それにしても、 “カガミ”なんて‥‥）
English: (It's kinda hard to believe everything's the fault of a mirror, but...)

Japanese: マメのオジさん。カガミに写った 姿を見ただけなの？
English: Do you think Old Seedy saw everything through a reflection...?

Japanese: そうなると‥‥あの証言に、 ムジュンがなくなるよ。
English: If he did, it would explain all the contradictions in his testimony.

Japanese: マコちゃんの立場が、 悪くなっちゃうね！
English: But that just makes the situation worse for Maggey!

Japanese: あのオジさんの証言‥‥ もっと、よく考えてみようよ！
English: There's gotta be something in that old man's testimony! We've just gotta dig deeper!

Japanese: その、“２人のお客さん” というのは‥‥？
English: And who were the two customers, exactly?

Japanese: モチロン、亡くなった 若い男のコと‥‥、
English: Mais, of course. La young man who died...

Japanese: もう若くない男のコの フ・タ・リ。
English: ...and la other, not-so-young, man.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥きのうの証人、ですか。
English: Hmm... You are referring to yesterday's witness, I presume?

Japanese: （マコちゃんが見た “もう１人の客”‥‥）
English: (What about the other man Maggey says she saw at the table?)

Japanese: （やはり、その存在を認める つもりは、ないみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Something tells me Mr. Armstrong isn't planning to disclose his existence...)

Japanese: 何か、証拠がないとねえ‥‥。
English: We need some hard evidence first before we can bring him up, don't we...?

Japanese: しかたない。他のところを 攻めるとするか。
English: I guess I'll just have to try a different approach for the time being.

Japanese: い‥‥インテリアの‥‥？ 初めて聞きましたけど‥‥
English: You were experimenting with art deco? How come I never heard about that before today?

Japanese: あら。そうだったかしら。
English: You are not familiar w'iz z'e language of interior design, monsieur?

Japanese: どうして今まで、 ダマっていたんですか！
English: Please stay on topic! Now, why didn't you tell the court about this before!?

Japanese: おかしいわねえ。まっ先に 話したつもりだったのに‥‥
English: But I did. Just a few moments ago!

Japanese: ちょっと、いいですかな。
English: Ahem! Excuse me, Mr. Armstrong.

Japanese: その。インテリア、というのは やはり、インテリゲンチャの‥‥
English: This "deco" you mentioned... Are you referring to some sort of "decocture"?

Japanese: いえいえ。インテリアというのは、 “内装”のコトです。
English: No, no! Art deco, it is a style of design! ...Your Honor.

Japanese: カベとか床のジュータンとか、 家具とか‥‥
English: He's talking about interior design. Walls, ceilings, carpets, that kind of thing.

Japanese: ‥‥ああ、はいはい。 そっちですか。
English: Ah, yes. Of course. That "deco".

Japanese: もうちょっと、オトメな感じに したくって。
English: I was trying to achieve a more "la effeminate" look for my restaurant.

Japanese: ダイタンに模様がえ しようと思ったのよぉ。
English: I was planning z'e most bold remodeling of la decor.

Japanese: “大きなカガミ”‥‥というのは、 どれぐらいの？
English: How big of a mirror are we talking about here?

Japanese: はばが４メートル、高さは‥‥そう ２メートルぐらい、かしら。
English: Bof! Something about 4 meters wide and, err... Oui. About 2 meters 'igh.
(Let's see... If 1 meter is about 1 yard... Holy glass in a frame, that's huge!)

Japanese: 天井に、バーンと はってやろうかと思ったワケね。
English: I was intending to install it on la ceiling eventually.

Japanese: て、天井‥‥ですか？ （カガミなんて、あったっけ？）
English: The ceiling!? (Was there a mirror on the ceiling!? I don't remember...)

Japanese: まあ‥‥ケッキョク、 やめちゃったんだけど。
English: Mais, non. But I decided not to go through w'iz it in z'e end.

Japanese: （どうする？　もっと詳しく 聞いてみるか‥‥？）
English: (What should I do? Should I ask him more about the mirror or not?)

Japanese: （そこまで大きなカガミについて ウソをつくはずがない‥‥）
English: (He couldn't lie about such a huge object like that.)

Japanese: （きっと、カガミは店内に あったんだろう）
English: (It must've been there in the restaurant somewhere.)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。大きなカガミに、 被害者の姿が写った‥‥と。
English: Hmm... So the witness yesterday had seen the victim reflected in a mirror...

Japanese: そんな大きなカガミがあったら、 だれかが証言するハズです！
English: If you really had such a large mirror in the restaurant, someone would have noticed it!

Japanese: 五十嵐さんも、被告人も‥‥ ヒトコトも言っていません！
English: But there's nothing about a mirror in Mr. Kudo or Maggey Byrde's testimonies!

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: B-But...

Japanese: ‥‥聞かなかったアンタが ワルいぜ、まるほどう‥‥
English: You didn't ask, Trite. You have only yourself to blame for such sloppy work.

Japanese: なんですって‥‥
English: What!?

Japanese: 事件の前日‥‥、たしかに カガミは吐麗美庵に届いている。
English: A mirror was delivered to Trés Bien the day before the incident.
R-Really?

Japanese: インテリアの改装は、たしかに 行われたのさ。
English: As Mr. Armstrong testified, he was carrying out some design changes.

Japanese: まあ‥‥そのカガミは 使われなかったようだが。
English: And as it turned out, he didn't actually use the mirror in the end.

Japanese: （‥‥アヤしすぎるな‥‥）
English: (This just doesn't add up.)

Japanese: （たとえ、カガミが吐麗美庵に 届けられていたとしても‥‥）
English: (Even if a mirror was delivered to Trés Bien...)

Japanese: （それが店内にあったかどうかは 立証されていない！）
English: (...it doesn't prove that it was in the restaurant on the day of the crime.)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　ニラむなら、 カガミの中の男にすることだ。
English: Ha...! If you want to doubt someone, Trite, look in the mirror.

Japanese: ‥‥きっと、アンタをニラみ返して くれるコトだろうぜ‥‥。
English: I'm sure the person looking back at you will be dubious enough!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。大きなカガミに、 被害者の姿が写った‥‥と。
English: Hmm... So the witness yesterday had seen the victim reflected in a mirror...

Japanese: ふつう‥‥気がつきませんか？ カガミなんですから。
English: Normally, I'd expect people to know the difference between a reflection and a real object.

Japanese: 《ふつう》‥‥か。そんな コトバに、なんの重みがある？
English: "Normally"? How does normality come into this?

Japanese: コトなかれ主義者の 泣きゴトよりタチが悪いぜ。
English: That's lame, Trite. Even for you.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことですか？
English: Huh?

Japanese: “ふつう、あり得ない” ‥‥そんなコトを言い出したら、
English: Are you trying to say that if something isn't normal, it isn't possible? Is that it?

Japanese: そんなギザギザな弁護士や、 チョンマゲのおじょうちゃん‥‥
English: Where does that leave the porcu-headed lawyer and the topknot chick over there...

Japanese: それに、こんな仮面をつけた 検事に‥‥居場所なんかねえぜッ！
English: ...and the ungodly cool guy with the mask over here? Well, Trite!?

Japanese: ぐ‥‥ッ！
English: Aaack! Not the hair!

Japanese: いくらなんでも チョンマゲはないでしょッ！
English: I do not have a topknot!

Japanese: まあ‥‥たしかに。
English: Mr. Godot is correct.

Japanese: “ふつう”というコトバは、 なんのコンキョにも、なりません。
English: A lack of normality is no basis for discounting an argument.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥これで、 スジはとおったワケよねえ‥‥。
English: Bien! Logic 'as won la day!

Japanese: 見たもの、ゼンブ‥‥ですか。
English: Everything? He would have seen everything in reverse?

Japanese: ウイ。
English: Oui.

Japanese: ‥‥マメのオジさんの証言、 もう一度、思い出してみようよ！
English: Hey, Nick... We should take a sec and think about what Old Seedy said in his testimony...

Japanese: （あのおジイさんが、セイカクに なんと言ったか‥‥？）
English: (How did he phrase it again...?)

Japanese: ヤツは、メガネをかけたほうの耳に ラジオのイヤホンをハメとった！
English: The boy was wearing the earpiece on the same side as the green lens of his specs.

Japanese: まちがいないぞ！　ブチこんで やがったのさ。左の耳にな！
English: No question! You can lock me up if I'm wrong! It was his left ear, without a doubt!

Japanese: そしてそのまま、その手で コーシーを飲んだ。左手でなッ！
English: And then he used the same hand to pick up the cup. His left hand!

Japanese: ‥‥これが、カガミに写っていた 姿だったのなら‥‥
English: If he saw everything he described reflected in a mirror...

Japanese: “左”だったのが、みんな“右”に なるワケだよね。
English: ...then everything he said he saw on the "left" was actually on the "right", huh.

Japanese: （それで‥‥この証言、おかしな ところはなくなるのか‥‥？）
English: (And that clears up all the problems with his testimony, I guess... Or does it...?)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
カップに、イヤホン‥‥ そして、メガネ、と。
English: The coffee cup, the earpiece, and the HMD...

Japanese: ‥‥イガラシさんの証言を 思い出していただきましょう。
English: Let's think back over Mr. Kudo's testimony for a second, shall we?

Japanese: ヤツは、メガネをかけたほうの耳に ラジオのイヤホンをハメとった！
English: The boy was wearing the earpiece on the same side as the green lens of his specs.

Japanese: まちがいないぞ！　ブチこんで やがったのさ。左の耳にな！
English: No question! You can lock me up if I'm wrong! It was his left ear, without a doubt!

Japanese: この証言をまとめると‥‥
English: So, to summarize...

Japanese: モノクルとイヤホンは、両方とも 左耳につけていたことになります。
English: ...we were told both the HMD and the earpiece were on the victim's left side.

Japanese: そして、それがカガミに 写ったのならば‥‥
English: Now, if Mr. Kudo saw all that as a reflection in a mirror...

Japanese: モノクルとイヤホンは‥‥ 右耳に見えたはずです。
English: ...it means both the HMD and the earpiece were actually on the victim's right side.

Japanese: トレ・ビアン！ やればできるじゃない！
English: Exactement! You see, monsieur. Now z'at you think about it, it is not so 'ard, non?

Japanese: しかし‥‥ここに大きな ムジュンが生まれるのです。
English: Unfortunately, that's where we run into a monumental contradiction with the facts!

Japanese: もしイガラシさんが、カガミに 写った被害者を見たのならば‥‥
English: If Mr. Kudo really did see everything in a mirror...

Japanese: なぜ、このモノクルだけが 左右、逆になっていなかったのか！
English: ...why is it that the HMD is now on the wrong side of his head!?

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！ ‥‥そのとおりです！
English: Order! Order! ...Mr. Wright is correct!

Japanese: この証人が目撃したのが、 カガミに写った姿ならば‥‥
English: If the witness genuinely observed the victim reflected in a mirror...

Japanese: 被害者は、右の耳に メガネをつけているハズです！
English: ...then we would expect the victim's eyepiece to have been over his right eye.

Japanese: ‥‥苦ェ。
English: ...How bitter.

Japanese: アンタも早く、この味がわかる オトナになることだ。まるほどう。
English: Trite, you should have a taste of this bitterness. It'll calm you down in no time.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか？
English: A-Are we talking about your coffee... or something completely different...?

Japanese: アンタは、この証人の “性格”がわかってねえのさ。
English: You don't understand the way the witness thinks.

Japanese: セイカク‥‥ですか？
English: How he thinks?

Japanese: コイツ‥‥おぼえてるだろう？
English: You remember this, I presume?

Japanese: 《花ビンを割っちゃって ごめんなさい》‥‥ですか。
English: The "I broke the vase... sorry" apology lett-- I mean Mr. Kudo's sworn testimony?

Japanese: そう‥‥あのジイさんには、 １つのクセがあるのさ。
English: Exactly. The old man has one very grievous habit... other than throwing seeds.

Japanese: 《印象に残ったコトほど、 カンちがいしてキオクする》‥‥
English: The more of an impression something makes, the more muddled his mind makes it.

Japanese: この被害者のツラ‥‥ イチバン、目立つのはどこだ？
English: And what's the most striking thing about Mr. Elg?

Japanese: それはやはり、‥‥その 《ものくる》でしょうな。
English: Clearly, it's the victim's eyepiece.

Japanese: そう‥‥。ジイさん、また やっちまったのさ。
English: And that's my point. The old man strikes again!

Japanese: 《モノクル》が、あまりに 強く、印象に残った。だから‥‥
English: Mr. Elg's HMD made a big impression on the old man.

Japanese: 『ワシはイヤホンといっしょに、 イカれたメガネも見たんだ！』
English: "I saw the earpiece and those new-fangled spectacles he was wearing!"

Japanese: 『そうだよ！　いっしょくたに ブチこんでんだ！　左耳にッ！』
English: "Oh, yes! They were both on his left ear! Do you hear? His left ear!"

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ ‥‥どうだ？　まるほどう。
English: Ha...! Well, Trite?

Japanese: ぐ‥‥。 （ジイさんのモノマネか？　今の）
English: Urk... (That's the worst, but best impression of Kudo ever...)

Japanese: モノマネ、じょうずだね。 ゴドー検事。
English: Wow! I really thought he was Old Seedy for a minute there! Godot's good!

Japanese: そこまで！
English: Enough!

Japanese: たしかに、きのうの証人は 早とちりの多いヒトでしたね。
English: I must agree that yesterday's witness was irresponsibly rash in much of his testimony.

Japanese: せっかくのムジュンが 《早とちり》あつかいされてるよ！
English: Bad luck, Nick. Looks like the "boil" of a contradiction you found is just a "rash".

Japanese: ‥‥カガミはマメを食わねえ。 そして、ウソもつかねえのさ。
English: A mirror can't be beaten by a handful of seeds, nor can it lie.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥ジッサイ、ジイさんが 見たカラクリは、なんだったのか？
English: So... What exactly was the old man looking at?

Japanese: アンタは、そいつを知っている。
English: Fill us in, Mr. Armstrong.

Japanese: そいつを、話してみな。 ‥‥聞いてやるぜ。
English: Go on, tell the court. We're all ears.

Japanese: ウイ。お店の上面図を見ながら 聞いてちょうだいな。
English: Oui. I can explain. Please, if you will look at la plans of la restaurant.

Japanese: ～“カガミ”について～
English: -- The Mirror --

Japanese: じゃ、みなさん。 用意はいいかしらあ？
English: Alors. Is everyone sitting comfortably?

Japanese: カガミは、テーブルの列を２つに わけるように置いてあったの。
English: La mirror, it was in z'e middle of la restaurant, dividing z'e two 'alves.

Japanese: カガミに写った被害者を見ることが できる席は、ひとつしかないわね。
English: Z'ere is only one seat from which you could 'ave seen an image of la victim.

Japanese: それは、被害者のトナリよお。 そこが、オジさんの席だったのね！
English: Z'at was la seat at z'e table next to la victim's. Z'at was where la old man was sitting.

Japanese: 事件が起こって、さすがにジャマ だったから、カガミをどかしたの。
English: After la terrible incident occurred, I moved la mirror so it was not in z'e way.

Japanese: でも、他のモノにはいっさい、 手を触れていないわあ。
English: But naturally, I did not touch anything else!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。たしかに、今の説明に モンダイはないようです。
English: Hmm... I see no problems with the explanation we have just heard.

Japanese: 被害者のトナリの席なら、カガミに 写った姿を見ることができますね。
English: From the table next to the victim's, Mr. Kudo could have seen the victim in the mirror.

Japanese: ワタシ、ツミ作りなオンナね。 オトコたちを、まどわせちゃって。
English: What a naughty little coquette I am, confusing all z'e men like z'is!

Japanese: シンパイいらねえさ。
English: Don't worry about it.

Japanese: まどわされたのは、そこで冷や汗を 流してるダレかさんだけ、だぜ。
English: We can keep up, except for the guy breaking out in a cold sweat over there again.

Japanese: （くそお‥‥）
English: (Argh! I hate that guy!)

Japanese: “カガミ以外のものには いっさい、手を触れていない”
English: You said you didn't touch anything else apart from the mirror.

Japanese: ‥‥まちがいありませんな？
English: Are you quite sure about that?

Japanese: ヴォロンティエ！ ‥‥モチロンよお。
English: Volontiers! Of course!

Japanese: それでは、弁護人！ 尋問をおねがいします！
English: Very well. Mr. Wright. Your cross-examination, if you please.

Japanese: ～“カガミ”について～
English: -- The Mirror --

Japanese: カガミは、テーブルの列を２つに わけるように置いてあったの。
English: La mirror, it was in z'e middle of la restaurant, dividing z'e two 'alves.

Japanese: カガミに写った被害者を見ることが できる席は、ひとつしかないわね。
English: Z'ere is only one seat from which you could 'ave seen an image of la victim.

Japanese: それは、被害者のトナリよお。 そこが、オジさんの席だったのね！
English: Z'at was la seat at z'e table next to la victim's. Z'at was where la old man was sitting.

Japanese: 事件が起こって、さすがにジャマ だったから、カガミをどかしたの。
English: After la terrible incident occurred, I moved la mirror so it was not in z'e way.

Japanese: でも、他のモノにはいっさい、 手を触れていないわあ。
English: But naturally, I did not touch anything else!

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥おかしいよね？
English: It is pretty strange, though, isn't it?

Japanese: そんなにおっきなカガミのコト、 ダレもなんにも言わない、なんて。
English: I mean, nobody mentioned anything about a really large mirror.

Japanese: マコちゃんも、 マメのオジさんも‥‥
English: You'd think someone would've, but Maggey didn't, and neither did Old Seedy.

Japanese: “吐麗美庵の店内に、カガミなんか なかった‥‥”
English: Then the only logical explanation is...

Japanese: たしかに、そう考えるほうが 自然だな。
English: ...that there was no mirror inside Trés Bien that day.

Japanese: （なんとかして、立証しよう！ 店内に、カガミはなかった‥‥）
English: (Now I've just got to prove it... Somehow...)

Japanese: 上面図でカクニンすると‥‥ ここですね？
English: So, run this by me again. The mirror was here, correct?

Japanese: ウイ。
English: Oui.

Japanese: “うい”じゃありませんよ！
English: Oui? Really!?

Japanese: なんでこんなジャマなところに カガミなんか‥‥！
English: Because I know if I were you, I wouldn't have put a mirror there! It would be in the way!

Japanese: ‥‥アンタに“ジャマ”を 語る資格はねえぜ。
English: Look who's talking, Trite.

Japanese: はい‥‥？
English: Huh?

Japanese: 目ざわりなところに 立ちやがって‥‥
English: You're obstructing my view, among other things.

Japanese: いやいや！　ここ、 ぼくの席ですから‥‥！
English: But, but, but! This is my seat in the courtroom...!

Japanese: あのレストランの基本スタイルは 『客がいない』ってトコだ。
English: Trés Bien's "charm" is that it gives you the impression that you're the only customer.

Japanese: ‥‥一時的にカガミがあっても、 ジャマにはならねえさ。
English: Temporarily placing a mirror in that spot would hardly be "in the way".

Japanese: アンタとちがってな。 ‥‥まるほどう。
English: Unlike you... Trite.

Japanese: とにかく、カガミは 置いてあったワケなのよぉ。
English: I tell you, monsieur, la mirror was z'ere, in z'e middle of la restaurant.

Japanese: それは、どこですか！
English: And where would that be!?

Japanese: あらあら。そんなに 身を乗り出しちゃって。
English: Oh là là! Look 'ow you lean towards me!

Japanese: おネエさん、年下キラーだから‥‥ もっと、ジラしちゃおうかしら。
English: I always attract z'e younger boys! Maybe I should keep you in suspense a little longer!

Japanese: 証人！　知っているコトは さっさと証言するように！
English: Mr. Armstrong! Tell the court what you know at once!

Japanese: おネエさん、オジサマキラーだし。 さらにジラしちゃおうかしら。
English: I attract z'e older ones, too, you know, 'andsome! Shall I tease you, too!?

Japanese: むぐぐぐ。
English: Nnngh...

Japanese: オレにはアツいコイビトがいる。 ‥‥いくらでも、待つぜ。
English: I'm already seeing a very hot someone, so I'm afraid you'll be waiting for a long time.

Japanese: アツいコーヒー飲んでるよ‥‥。
English: I bet she has mocha-cream skin and cappuccino perfume.

Japanese: カガミに写った被害者を見るコトが できるテーブル‥‥
English: Bien. I will tell you... Z'ere was only one seat from which you could 'ave seen...

Japanese: どうして、そういうコトに なるんですか？
English: So why can you only see the victim from that particular seat?

Japanese: だ・か・ら。‥‥上面図を 見れば、わかるでしょお？
English: Mais, monsieur. It is obvious, non? If you look at la plans you will understand.

Japanese: 被害者の姿は、こんなふうに カガミに反射するワケだから‥‥
English: La victim would 'ave been reflected in la mirror like so.

Japanese: ホラ。トナリにいれば、 ハッキリ見えるじゃない！
English: If you were sitting at la table next to 'im, you would see everything, non?

Japanese: マメのオジさん、あの席に 座ってたワケだね。
English: So that's the seat Old Seedy was sitting in on that day...

Japanese: ‥‥事件が起こったとき。
English: ...when the poisoning happened.

Japanese: （オジさんが、被害者のトナリに 座っていた‥‥）
English: (The old man was sitting at the table next to the victim?)

Japanese: （なんか、おかしくないか‥‥？）
English: (Why does that seem kinda odd...?)

Japanese: それは‥‥イガラシさんが 警察を呼びに行っているあいだに？
English: Did you move the mirror while Mr. Kudo was off calling the police?

Japanese: そうよお。お店のソトに 運びだしたわけ。
English: Oui. Exactement. I carried it out of la restaurant z'en.

Japanese: そんなデカいカガミ、ひとりで 持てたんですか？
English: You moved a huge mirror like that all by yourself?

Japanese: おネエさん、モテモテだから。
English: What can I say? I know 'ow to pick things up, 'andsome!

Japanese: ククッ‥‥。
English: Heh heh...

Japanese: ご‥‥ゴドー検事が笑ったよ！
English: G-Godot actually laughed at something!

Japanese: まあ‥‥‥、その体格なら 可能でしょうなあ。
English: Well, given the witness's physique, I suppose it is possible.

Japanese: 証人。他に、現場から 動かしたモノはありますかな？
English: Did you move anything else from the crime scene, Mr. Armstrong?

Japanese: セワ好きなオンナに 見えるでしょ？　ワタシ。
English: I look like z'e obliging type, non?

Japanese: まちがいありませんね？
English: Are you sure about that?

Japanese: カガミをどかした以外は、 何も触ってないの。
English: I touched nothing except la mirror!

Japanese: 弁護人？　何か現場に、おかしな 点でもあるのですかな？
English: Mr. Wright? Is there something the witness has said that doesn't match the crime scene?

Japanese: （ハッキリしないんだけど‥‥ 何かモヤモヤするような‥‥）
English: (Yeah, there is... I just can't put my finger on what exactly.)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　弁護士さんは ムネヤケ‥‥みたいだぜ。
English: Ha...! Suffering from a case of heartburn, Trite?

Japanese: そんなときはコレ。ローズマリーと ミルラとレモンのエッセンスを‥‥
English: Oh, I 'ave just z'e thing for z'at! An oil with golden myrrh and frankenstaince...

Japanese: コーヒーにいれて、 飲んでみるがいいぜ。
English: Add a few drops to your coffee, and voila! Enjoy.

Japanese: （とにかく、何か証拠を 探すしかない、か‥‥）
English: (Focus, Phoenix. Breathe... I need to ignore those two and just find some evidence.)

Japanese: 裁判長！　今の証言は‥‥ この証拠品とムジュンしています！
English: This piece of evidence contradicts with testimony we have heard, Your Honor!

Japanese: これは‥‥現場写真ですか‥‥？
English: The crime photo...?

Japanese: ここには‥‥本来、“あるべきで ないもの”が写っているのです。
English: Yes. This photo clearly shows something that theoretically should not exist!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: What on Earth do you mean by that, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: “あるべきでないもの”同士、 ピンと来るモノがあるんだろうぜ。
English: "Should not exist"? Ha... Sounds like you're describing yourself, Trite.

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 示していただきましょうか。
English: Now then, if the defense would please clarify its statement.

Japanese: この写真に“あるべきでない もの”‥‥とは！
English: What is the something that "should not exist" in this photo?

Japanese: “あるべきでないもの” ‥‥もちろん、コレです！
English: This is what should not be in the picture!

Japanese: ‥‥どれ、ですかな？ よくわかりませんでした。
English: I'm sorry? I'm afraid you've lost me.
Um...

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　しかたねえ。 オレが教えてやろうか‥‥
English: Ha...! I suppose it's up to me to clarify the defense's claim.

Japanese: “あるべきでないもの” ‥‥それは‥‥ッ！
English: The something that "shouldn't exist" is clearly...

Japanese: や、やっぱり 考えなおします！
English: W-Wait, wait, wait! I've change my mind, Your Honor!

Japanese: （コーヒーの雨に打たれそうな 予感がする‥‥）
English: (The last thing I need right now is a scalding hot coffee shower...)

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、気を取りなおして。
English: Very well. Gather your thoughts, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 証人。もう一度、最初から 証言をおねがいします。
English: In the meantime, the witness will repeat his testimony for the court.

Japanese: ウイ。よろこんで‥‥！
English: Oui. I would be delighted!

Japanese: モチロン、これです！ “あるべきでないもの”‥‥
English: I think it's pretty obvious that this is what should not be in the picture!

Japanese: か‥‥花ビン、ですか‥‥？
English: The vase!?

Japanese: し、しかし‥‥いったい、 なんのカンケイが‥‥
English: What possible connection does that have with this witness's testimony?

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。
English: Your Honor...

Japanese: このテーブルに、花ビンが あってはならないのです。
English: I'm telling you that there should have been no vase on this table.

Japanese: ‥‥この証拠と、アキラカに ムジュンしますからね！
English: Because it very clearly contradicts with this piece of evidence!

Japanese: ‥‥いかがですか！
English: Well, Your Honor?

Japanese: そうですなあ‥‥。
English: Well...

Japanese: “アキラカなムジュン”と 言ったワリには‥‥
English: ...I'm not sure the contradiction...

Japanese: そうでもなかったですね。
English: ...is as clear as you would have us believe, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
次回チャレンジするときは、 そのへんを考えるように。
English: Perhaps you should reconsider before you try again, hm?

Japanese: じ‥‥“次回”‥‥？ （ハズレ‥‥ってコトか‥‥）
English: Try again...? (You mean I was wrong?)

Japanese: 裁判長！　今の証言‥‥ この証拠品とムジュンしています！
English: Your Honor! Mr. Kudo's words yesterday strongly contradict Mr. Armstrong's testimony!

Japanese: それは‥‥きのうのダメ証人の 反省文ですか。
English: This is... the letter of apology that was written by Mr. Kudo, is it not?

Japanese: ‥‥こんなシロモノですが、 いちおう、証言書です。
English: I realize it looks useless, Your Honor, but this is still testimony.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　“ダメ”同士、 ピンと来るモノがあるんだろうぜ。
English: Ha...! I guess useless people are only really good at identifying useless things.

Japanese: それで‥‥この紙キレが、何か？
English: What relevance does this scrap of paper have to the trial, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 裁判長！　五十嵐さんの証言には、 重要なイミがあったのです！
English: Mr. Kudo's testimony is actually very relevant to the question at hand, Your Honor!

Japanese: きのうの証言で ハッキリしたコトは、ひとつ。
English: There is one thing that was clearly demonstrated by yesterday's testimony.

Japanese: イガラシさんは、自分の席の 花ビンを割った、ということです。
English: Mr. Kudo broke the vase that was on the table where he was sitting.

Japanese: しかし！　被害者のトナリの テーブルには‥‥
English: And yet!

Japanese: このとおり、ちゃんと 花ビンはある！　‥‥つまり‥‥
English: As the court can see, there is an unbroken vase on the table next to the victim! Why?

Japanese: イガラシさんの席は、被害者の トナリではなかったのです！
English: Because Mr. Kudo was not, in fact, sitting at the table next to the victim at all!

Japanese: ‥‥バカな！　あり得ねえ！
English: Don't be an idiot, Trite! That's impossible!

Japanese: それ以外の席で、カガミに写った 被害者を見ることは不可能だぜ！
English: That seat's the only one Kudo could've seen the victim's reflection from!

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおり！
English: Exactly!

Japanese: 結論は‥‥ただひとつ。
English: There is only one conclusion we can draw from this contradiction.

Japanese: あの日、吐麗美庵に カガミなど存在しなかった！
English: There was no mirror in Trés Bien that day!

Japanese: 本土坊さん。あなたの証言は まったくの大ウソです！
English: Your testimony, Mr. Armstrong, is an elaborate lie!

Japanese: きゃああああああああああああッ！
English: Mon dieeeeeeeu!

Japanese: ‥‥割れた花ビンなんざ、 かたづけちまったんだろうぜ。
English: Don't try to confuse the court, Trite! Obviously, the witness cleaned up the vase...

Japanese: 警察の連中が、現場を探して モタモタしているあいだになァ！
English: ...while the police were taking their time getting to the crime scene!

Japanese: ザンネンながら、 そうはいきません！
English: Unfortunately, Mr. Godot, that doesn't quite work for me.

Japanese: 本土坊さんは、たしかに こう証言している！
English: Mr. Armstrong already testified to the contrary. In his own words...

Japanese: 『カガミ以外のものには 手を触れなかった』‥‥と！
English: "I did not touch anything else" except the mirror!

Japanese: う‥‥‥‥‥‥ クオオオオオオオオオオオオオッ！
English: Ur... urgh! Gaaaaaaaaargh!

Japanese: 証人！　どうなのですかッ？
English: Well, witness? What do you have to say for yourself?

Japanese: う‥‥ううううう‥‥
English: *sniffle*

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥店内にカガミなんか なかったんだ！）
English: (I was right! There was no mirror in the restaurant that day!)

Japanese: しかし‥‥そうなると、最初の ギモンに逆戻りです。
English: In light of this revelation, we return back to the original problem.

Japanese: 被害者はなぜ、聞こえないはずの 左の耳にイヤホンをつけていたか？
English: Why did the victim have an earpiece in an ear in which he couldn't hear?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
人生は一度きり、真剣勝負だ。 《逆戻り》なんて、認めねえぜ。
English: You only get one shot in life. There's no turning back.

Japanese: カガミを否定するのなら‥‥ まるほどう。
English: If you want to claim that the mirror wasn't there, Trite...

Japanese: ‥‥アンタに 答えてもらうとするぜ！
English: ...then this problem is all yours.

Japanese: あのジイさんが見たのは、 いったい、なんだったのか！
English: How do you explain what the old man saw?

Japanese: （このナゾのコタエは‥‥ 真相に一歩、大きく近づく！）
English: (If I can answer this, then I'll be that much closer to the truth! I can feel it!)

Japanese: ‥‥だいじょうぶなの？ なるほどくん‥‥
English: ...Are you going to be OK? Can you really solve this contradiction, Nick?

Japanese: この事件では‥‥ “ムジュン”は、１つじゃない。
English: There's more than just this one contradiction, Maya.

Japanese: ど、どういうコト？ “１つじゃない”って‥‥
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: マコちゃんが見たことを、 思い出してみるんだ。
English: Remember what Maggey told us?

Japanese: “被害者のテーブルには、 もうひとりの客がいた”
English: "There was another man at the victim's table."

Japanese: “被害者のテーブルには、 サンプルのＣＤが置いてあった”
English: And, "there was a sample CD on the victim's table."

Japanese: ‥‥ことごとく、五十嵐さんの 証言と食いちがっている。
English: It all flies in the face of Mr. Kudo's testimony.

Japanese: （なぜ、こんなことが起こったか？ コタエは、１つしかあり得ない）
English: (And I think I know the reason why nothing in this case is adding up.)

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 あなたのコタエを聞きましょう。
English: Well, Mr. Wright? Let's hear your answer.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: イガラシさんが見た、“被害者”の ムジュン。‥‥その原因は‥‥！
English: The reason behind all the contradictions in Mr. Kudo's testimony is simple.

Japanese: カンタンに考えれば、証人の カンちがい、ではないでしょうか。
English: Clearly, Mr. Kudo made a mistake!

Japanese: まるほどう‥‥
English: Mr. Trite...

Japanese: そもそも、このムジュンを 持ち出したのは、キサマだぞ。
English: You're the one who brought up all these contradictions.
And...?

Japanese: ジイさんの証言を“カンちがい”で すませるならば‥‥
English: If you're trying to tell us the old man just made a mistake...

Japanese: そこのウエイトレスは、今すぐ 有罪じゃねえか！
English: ...we can wrap up this case right now with a guilty verdict!

Japanese: 今すぐ有罪に しておきましょうか？　弁護人。
English: How about it, Mr. Wright? Should I just declare your client guilty?

Japanese: カンタンに考えすぎだよ、 なるほどくん！
English: Is that the best you can come up with, Nick?

Japanese: す、スミマセン。 カンちがいは、ぼくでした‥‥
English: I-I'm sorry, Your Honor. I was the one who made a mistake.

Japanese: もう一度！　よく考えてください！
English: I'll give you one more chance, Mr. Wright. Think carefully before you answer this time!

Japanese: ここは、発想を逆転させて‥‥ 耳鼻科のカンちがいだったのでは！
English: I believe we're looking at this the wrong way. It was actually the doctor's mistake.

Japanese: ‥‥はい？
English: What?

Japanese: つまり、左耳のコマクが破れて いたのは、医者のカンちがいで‥‥
English: Yes. The doctor got the wrong ear!

Japanese: 検死の結果は、きのうの裁判で 報告されたぜ、まるほどう‥‥
English: Well, I believe we saw an autopsy report yesterday.

Japanese: 左耳のコマクが破れて、ついでに クスリが検出された、と‥‥！
English: One that stated the victim's left eardrum was ruptured, and had medicine in it!

Japanese: あなたのコマクが破れていないか シンパイになってきました。
English: I'm beginning to wonder if it's not your eardrum that's ruptured, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: こんなところで発想を 逆転して、どうするの！
English: This is no time to be playing with people's perceptions, Nick!

Japanese: この事件は‥‥フシギなほどに 食いちがいが多い。
English: This case is riddled with contradictions.

Japanese: それらのムジュンを 解決するコタエは、たった１つ。
English: Yet, there is one very simple answer that clears them all up.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥？
English: A-And what is that?

Japanese: 五十嵐さんの目撃した“事件”と、 被告人が体験した“事件”‥‥
English: The incident Mr. Kudo witnessed, and the incident the victim experienced...

Japanese: この２つは、まったく 別のモノだったのです！
English: ...were two completely different events!

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと‥‥
English: What!?

Japanese: イガラシさんの見た“被害者”は、 “岡　高夫”ではなかった！
English: Yes! The "victim" that Mr. Kudo saw wasn't Mr. Glen Elg at all!

Japanese: その“フリ”をした別人‥‥ ニセモノだったのです！
English: It was an imposter; a phony pretending to be Mr. Elg!

Japanese: 当然、《ニセモノ》のコマクは 破れていなかった！
English: Obviously, unlike the victim, there was nothing wrong with the imposter's left eardrum.

Japanese: だから！　左耳にイヤホンを つけてしまったのです！
English: That's how he ended up wearing the earpiece in his left ear by mistake!

Japanese: ぶぐほほおおおおッ！
English: Phwoooooooh!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ 静まらなければ、退廷を命じます！
English: Order! Order in the court! Settle down, or I'll clear the courtroom!

Japanese: カンカンうるせえぜ、ジイさん。 アンタが退廷しな！
English: Quiet, gramps! Why don't you clear out of here, huh?

Japanese: ええええええッ！
English: WHAT DID YOU SAY!?

Japanese: ジイさんが見たのは‥‥ ニセの事件だった、ってのか？
English: Trite... Are you saying that what Mr. Kudo saw was a set-up?

Japanese: ‥‥そうです。
English: Yes.

Japanese: だれかが岡　高夫になりすまして、 コーヒーで倒れるシバイをした。
English: Someone pretended to be Glen Elg and acted out the whole coffee poisoning.

Japanese: もちろん、五十嵐　将兵に 見せるために！
English: All for the express purpose of creating a "witness" out of one Mr. Victor Kudo!

Japanese: バカげてるぜ！ いったい‥‥なんのためだ！
English: Get real, Trite! Why would anyone want to do that?

Japanese: 決まってるじゃないですか。
English: Isn't it obvious?

Japanese: 五十嵐さんの証言で、最も 重要なポイントは‥‥
English: The thing Mr. Kudo was most insistent about in his testimony was...

Japanese: 女給さんがコーヒーを運んできた のだが‥‥中に、何か入れたのだ！
English: The serving girl brought him a javaccino... But she put something in it!

Japanese: 女給さんの正体こそ、被告席の 婦女子。よぉく、おぼえておる！
English: That's the serving girl, right there in the defendant's chair. I remember her well!

Japanese: ま‥‥まさか‥‥
English: I-It's so hard to believe, but...

Japanese: イガラシさんに、ニセモノの 《事件》を見せた理由は、１つ！
English: There was one, and only one reason to show Mr. Kudo this fake poisoning!

Japanese: 被告人・須々木　マコの 《犯行の瞬間》を見せるためです！
English: To show Maggey Byrde in the act of poisoning the coffee!

Japanese: その《須々木　マコ》も ニセモノだった、ってワケかい‥‥
English: Are you insinuating that the waitress in the old man's story was a fake as well?

Japanese: たしかに、店内に他の客は いなかった。‥‥しかし‥‥
English: It's true that there were no other customers in the restaurant at the time, but...

Japanese: シェフはいた！ コイツが気づかないハズはねえ。
English: ...it's also true that the chef was there! He would have noticed what was happening.

Japanese: そ‥‥そのとおりです！ 証人！　いかがですか！
English: Th-That's right! Well, witness?

Japanese: 店内で、そんなシバイが 行われていたのなら‥‥
English: If your restaurant really was the scene of such theatrics...

Japanese: あなたは、知っていたはずです！
English: ...you would have known about it, correct!?

Japanese: それは‥‥アレ。 ムズカシイ問題ね‥‥
English: Oh là là... Z'is is most difficult for me...

Japanese: いや、カンタンなコトです。 ‥‥証言してくれればいい。
English: No, it's quite simple. All you have to do is testify. You are under oath, after all.

Japanese: あの日、吐麗美庵の店内に 《ニセモノ》がいたかどうか‥‥？
English: Was there, in fact, a phony at Trés Bien that day!?

Japanese: その証言で、すべてが ハッキリするでしょう。
English: The defense demands that Mr. Armstrong tell the whole truth about what happened.

Japanese: 弁護人の要請を受けましょう。 証人に、証言を命じます！
English: The defense's request for additional testimony is accepted.

Japanese: 事件当日の、店内の状況を ‥‥細かく、セイカクに！
English: You will accurately explain, in detail, the events in the restaurant that day!
Ou... Oui.

Japanese: ～事件当時・店内の状況～
English: -- In the Restaurant --

Japanese: 被害者のムッシュ・タカオ‥‥ ヒトリで店に来たわ。
English: La victim, Monsieur Elg, 'e came to my restaurant alone.

Japanese: たしか‥‥そのすぐ後に、 あのオジさんも店に来たのよォ。
English: I remember la old man arrived not long after 'im.

Japanese: 店内には、他にお客さんは いなかったわ。
English: Z'ere were no other customers.

Japanese: 宝クジが当たって‥‥ ムッシュ・タカオは大騒ぎだった。
English: When 'e got word 'e won la lottery, Mon. Elg became very excited.

Japanese: その５分後ぐらいだったかしら。 あの事件が起こったのは。
English: It was approximately 5 minutes later z'at la poisoning incident occurred.

Japanese: ニセモノがシバイするヒマなんか なかったわねェ‥‥。
English: Non, z'ere was no time for a phony to do la acting.

Japanese: ‥‥ケッキョク、店内に カガミはなかったのですね？
English: ...Just so we're clear, there was no mirror in the restaurant, after all?

Japanese: ジュ・ヴ・デュマン・パルドン！ ユルしてちょうだいな！
English: Je vous demande pardon! Forgive me, Your Honor!

Japanese: お店のゴタゴタを早く 片づけたくて、あんなウソを‥‥
English: I lied because I wanted z'is mess to be cleared up quickly.

Japanese: あなたのしたことは、 《偽証罪》にあたります。
English: What you have just done is commit perjury, Mr. Armstrong!

Japanese: ‥‥処分は、あとで 言いわたしましょう。
English: I will decide how to punish you later.

Japanese: う‥‥ウイ。
English: O-Oui...

Japanese: それでは、弁護人！ 尋問をおねがいします！
English: For now, we will hear your cross-examination, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （本土坊さん‥‥。《偽証罪》で マイるのは早すぎるだろう）
English: (Hmm... He took that perjury charge a bit too well.)

Japanese: （この尋問が終わったら‥‥ それでは済まなくなるはずだ！）
English: (But I'm guessing he'll be in more serious trouble after this cross-examination!)

Japanese: ～事件当時・店内の状況～
English: -- In the Restaurant --

Japanese: 被害者のムッシュ・タカオ‥‥ ヒトリで店に来たわ。
English: La victim, Monsieur Elg, 'e came to my restaurant alone.

Japanese: たしか‥‥そのすぐ後に、 あのオジさんも店に来たのよォ。
English: I remember la old man arrived not long after 'im.

Japanese: 店内には、他にお客さんは いなかったわ。
English: Z'ere were no other customers.

Japanese: 宝クジが当たって‥‥ ムッシュ・タカオは大騒ぎだった。
English: When 'e got word 'e won la lottery, Mon. Elg became very excited.

Japanese: その５分後ぐらいだったかしら。 あの事件が起こったのは。
English: It was approximately 5 minutes later z'at la poisoning incident occurred.

Japanese: ニセモノがシバイするヒマなんか なかったわねェ‥‥。
English: Non, z'ere was no time for a phony to do la acting.

Japanese: 本土坊さん‥‥やっぱり、 事件に関係あるのかな。
English: I guess Mr. Armstrong is connected to this case, huh?

Japanese: まちがいないだろうね。
English: Absolutely.

Japanese: 岡　高夫のニセモノは存在した。 彼がそれを知らないハズがないよ。
English: Someone was impersonating Mr. Elg, and I refuse to believe he was oblivious.

Japanese: ‥‥ずっと、吐麗美庵に いたんだから。
English: He was there the whole time, after all.

Japanese: でも、それならマコちゃんだって 気がついたはずじゃないかな？
English: But if you're right, wouldn't Maggey have noticed, too?

Japanese: 彼女は‥‥キゼツしていただろう？ 事件が起こったときには。
English: She fell unconscious when the incident occurred, remember?

Japanese: あ！　じゃあ‥‥ ニセモノは、そのときに？
English: Ah! You mean that's when the phony staged his act?

Japanese: ‥‥この証言にムジュンを 見つけたときに、わかるよ。
English: We'll know for sure once I find a hole in this testimony.

Japanese: そのすぐ後、オジさんも来店を。 ２時、ちょっと過ぎぐらいかしら。
English: I remember la old man arrived not long after 'im, just after 2 PM.

Japanese: ‥‥そして、１人で テーブルについたわけですか？
English: Was he alone at his table as well?

Japanese: モチロンよお。 ワタシ、キッチンから見てたから。
English: Mais, oui. I saw 'im from la kitchen.

Japanese: ‥‥被告人の須々木　マコは、 ちがうものを見ています。
English: Yet the defendant, Ms. Byrde, remembers it differently.

Japanese: 被害者のテーブルには、 もう１人のオトコが‥‥
English: She swears there was another man at the victim's table.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　ザンネンだが まるほどう‥‥
English: Ha...! Unfortunately for you, Trite...

Japanese: 被害者が１人きりだったコトは、 きのうの証人も、断言してるぜ。
English: ...yesterday's witness also testified that the victim was alone.

Japanese: アンタのもがくサマを見てると ‥‥思い出しちまうぜ。
English: You know, seeing you squirm like that reminds me...

Japanese: ‥‥あまずっぱい、 あのコーヒーの味を。
English: ...of a certain coffee's bitter-sweet bite.

Japanese: （どんなコーヒーだよ！）
English: (What kind of coffee has he been drinking!?)

Japanese: コーヒーじゃなくて、コイだよね。 あまずっぱいのは。
English: It's not coffee, it's love! It's love that's bitter-sweet.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんも、そんなコトを言う トシゴロになったか‥‥）
English: (Hearing Maya say that makes her seem wise all of a sudden...)

Japanese: 五十嵐　将兵‥‥ですか？
English: By old man, you mean Victor Kudo, correct?

Japanese: ウチのコーヒーの味がわかる、 “通”なお客さんなのよォ。
English: Oui. 'E comes often for my special coffee.

Japanese: ‥‥オレも一度だけ、 あのコーヒーを飲んだコトがある。
English: I drank your coffee once, Mr. Armstrong.

Japanese: スゴい味だったな‥‥たしかに。 ヒトクチ飲む価値はあるぜ。
English: It's special, I'll give you that. It's worth a sip just for the experience.

Japanese: ウレシイわぁ。 また、飲みに来てね。
English: Oh, you make me so 'appy, monsieur! You are most welcome any time!

Japanese: ヒトクチ飲む価値はある。 しかし‥‥それ以上じゃねえさ。
English: I said it was worth one sip, and nothing more.

Japanese: （‥‥あのジイさん、被害者と ほぼ同時に、店に来たらしいな）
English: (So old Mr. Kudo arrived at the restaurant around the same time as the victim.)

Japanese: （もう少し、情報を 探ってみるか‥‥？）
English: (Maybe I should ask about his arrival in more detail.)

Japanese: （‥‥重要なのは、被害者の ニセモノの存在を暴くこと‥‥）
English: (All I need to do is establish that this phony victim really existed.)

Japanese: （ジイさんのことを聞いても、 コンランするだけだな）
English: (Asking more about the old man is only going to cloud the issue.)

Japanese: コーヒーの話はけっこうです。 ‥‥証言をつづけてください。
English: I think that's enough about coffee. Please continue with your testimony, Mr. Armstrong.

Japanese: アナタもヒトクチ飲みに来てね。 ウチのコーヒー‥‥。
English: You are welcome any time too, Monsieur Wright. I'll brew something special for you!

Japanese: 被害者が店に来た“すぐ後”に、 五十嵐さんも来たんですよね？
English: So you're saying that not much time elapsed between when the victim and Mr. Kudo arrived?

Japanese: そうよォ。
English: Oui. Z'at is correct.

Japanese: （その“スキ間”に 何かあったのかもしれない‥‥）
English: (But that still leaves the possibility that something happened in that gap of time.)

Japanese: どれぐらい後でしたか？ 被害者が来てから‥‥
English: And by your recollection, how much time would you say elapsed?

Japanese: そうねえ‥‥ ２分、ぐらいかしら。
English: Let me see... Approximately 2 minutes, I would say.

Japanese: にに、２分‥‥ですか。
English: T-Two minutes!? That's all?

Japanese: ふむう‥‥そんなに短い時間では、 何か細工をするのはムリですな。
English: Hmm... It seems unlikely that anything untoward could have happened in such a short time.

Japanese: （しまった‥‥！　深く追求 するべきじゃなかったか‥‥）
English: (Rats! I knew I shouldn't have pursued this line of questioning...)

Japanese: ところで‥‥オジさんが 店に来たのは、何時ごろでしたか？
English: Out of curiosity, about what time was it when Mr. Kudo arrived?

Japanese: そうねえ‥‥ よく、おぼえてないわぁ。
English: Oh, no... I cannot remember, monsieur.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。たしか、 きのうの証人によれば‥‥
English: Hmm... I believe we were told by a witness yesterday...

Japanese: 通報は、おっかないジイさんから、 ２時２５分に入ったッス。
English: The crime was reported at 2:25 PM by a kind of scary old man, sir.

Japanese: ‥‥というコトでしたが。
English: Does that perhaps jog your memory, witness?

Japanese: 来店から事件が起こるまで、 ２０分ぐらいだったから‥‥
English: La incident 'appened about 20 minutes after 'e arrived.

Japanese: ２時から２時１０分‥‥ ってコトになるかしらぁ。
English: So the victim must 'ave arrived between 2:00 PM and 2:10 PM, non?

Japanese: （２時すぎ、か‥‥）
English: (Hmm... Just after 2, huh...)

Japanese: オジさんのおかげで思い出せたワ。 ‥‥ア・リ・ガ・ト。
English: Thank you for your 'elp, in jogging my memory, monsieur. You are wonderful!

Japanese: ほっほっほっ。たまには 役に立たないと、アレですからな。
English: Hah hah hah! I can't sit here all the time and do nothing now, can I!?

Japanese: 記念に、その時間を証言に 加えていただきましょうかな。
English: The time of day will be added to the witness's testimony.

Japanese: ウイ、ムッシュ・サイバンチョ。 ‥‥いいわよぉ。
English: Oui, Monsieur Judge. Everything I do, I do it for you!

Japanese: メルシ・ビエン。‥‥なーんて。 ほっほっほっ。
English: Merci bien. That's French, isn't it? Hah hah hah!

Japanese: （裁判長、ゴキゲンだな‥‥）
English: (I'm glad at least one person is in a good mood...)

Japanese: ‥‥ハナ歌で シャンソンうたってるよ‥‥。
English: H-He's even humming a song to himself...!

Japanese: ということは、客は 被害者と、五十嵐さんだけ‥‥
English: So your only customers were Mr. Kudo and the victim?

Japanese: ‥‥何度、同じコトを聞けば 気が済むんだ、まるほどう‥‥
English: How many times do you need to ask the same thing, Trite?

Japanese: オレは、同じブレンドを２杯 飲むシュミはねえぜ。
English: You'd never catch me drinking the same blend twice!
Huh?

Japanese: あの店に、“ニセモノの被害者”が いたコトにしたいようだが‥‥
English: You're trying to establish the presence of a "phony victim" in the restaurant.

Japanese: そいつはムリ、ってモンだ。
English: But you're wasting your time.

Japanese: ハトのマメは、コーヒーには ならねえ‥‥そういうコトさ。
English: You can't grind bird seed to make coffee, if you catch my drift...

Japanese: （‥‥どこかに“穴”が あるはずなんだけど‥‥）
English: (But there's a hole in this testimony somewhere! I'm sure of it!)

Japanese: あなたも、それを見たんですか？
English: Did you see him?

Japanese: ワタシ、キッチンにいたんだけど。 騒ぎが聞こえたから。
English: Non, I was in ze kitchen. But I 'eard 'im.

Japanese: “５０００万円だッ！”みたいな。
English: I remember 'im shouting, "Yes! 'Alf a million bucks!"

Japanese: その騒ぎ‥‥トーゼン、 被告人も聞いていたハズだな？
English: Presumably the defendant heard that too, then, correct?

Japanese: そうねえ‥‥。
English: Maggey...?

Japanese: マコちゃん、マメ鉄砲を食らった ハトみたいなカオをしてたわ。
English: She looked like a poor little frightened dove!

Japanese: ちなみに、五十嵐さんは‥‥？
English: And what about Mr. Kudo?

Japanese: マメをノドにつめて、 目をシロクロさせてたわ。
English: La old man choked on some bird seed z'at got stuck in 'is throat.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥事件がもう１つ、 増えるところでしたな‥‥。
English: Hmm... It seems we now have yet another incident on our hands.

Japanese: そのとき、あなたは 何をしていたんですか？
English: And what were you doing at that point?

Japanese: お客もいなくて、 ヒマだったのでは‥‥
English: Without any customers, you must have had time to kill.

Japanese: ワタシ‥‥２つのカオを 持つオンナだから。
English: I am a multi-talented woman, monsieur!

Japanese: どういうコトですか‥‥？
English: Sorry? What do you mean?

Japanese: シェフの《本土坊　薫》と、 詩人の《クラリス・ホンドボー》。
English: Z'ere is la renowned chef, Jean Armstrong, and la tragic poet, Claurice Armstrong.

Japanese: くく、くらりす‥‥
English: C-C-Claurice...?

Japanese: ５０００万円の借金をしている ワタシが作った料理を食べた客が、
English: Oui, I was writing a poem. An angry tale of a chef in 'alf a million dollars of debt...

Japanese: ５０００万円のクジを当てた‥‥ その怒りを、詩にしていたの。
English: ...cooking for a man who won 'alf a million dollars on la lottery!

Japanese: “プルコワ　～なぜに？～” ‥‥ごヒロウしちゃおうかしら？
English: It is called, "Pourquoi". It means, "Why". Per'aps I could recite it for la court?

Japanese: ケッコウです。
English: Please don't...

Japanese: 事件が起こってから 警察への通報は、すぐに？
English: You mean you contacted the police as soon as the incident occurred?

Japanese: あのオジさんに、公衆電話から 通報してもらったわぁ。
English: I asked la old man to call from la pay phone.

Japanese: まるほどう。 アンタの主張では‥‥
English: By your own argument, Trite...

Japanese: ニセモノの“被害者”のシバイは、 あのジイさんに見せるためだった。
English: ...the purpose of this "phony victim's" performance was so the old man would see it.

Japanese: つまり‥‥
English: In other words...

Japanese: 事件が起こった後は、もう シバイを見せるチャンスは、ねえ。
English: ...once the incident occurred, this opportunity would completely disappear.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥そのとおりですな。
English: Indeed.

Japanese: どうかしら。ウタガイは 晴れたと思うケド‥‥？
English: Bien. It seems la shadow of doubt 'as been lifted, n'est-ce pas?

Japanese: その時間‥‥。ハッキリ おぼえてるんですか？
English: Are you absolutely sure about the time?

Japanese: よくよく考えてみれば‥‥ ２時を回っていたのは、たしかね。
English: When I think really 'ard, I am sure it was just after 2. Oui.

Japanese: ランチのサービス・タイムが 終わる時間なのよォ。
English: It is la time I stop serving la lunch menu.

Japanese: 私も、おヒルはランチ・サービスの 時間に食べることにしています。
English: Quite right. I always break for lunch when the restaurants are serving their specials.

Japanese: そのため、裁判を早く切り上げる コトも、あったりなかったり。
English: I've been known to wind up a case early just to make it on time!

Japanese: ほっほっ。
English: Hah hah!

Japanese: （ハラをすかせた裁判長は キケンだな‥‥）
English: (I guess you should never get between a hungry judge and his lunch...)

Japanese: おや。そういえば‥‥ そろそろ、おヒルですかな。
English: Oh, would you look at that! It's almost lunchtime already.

Japanese: 証人！　‥‥急いで証言を つづけてください！
English: Witness! Get on with your testimony, please!

Japanese: ついに‥‥つかまえましたよ、 本土坊さん。
English: I'm afraid I've finally got you, Mr. Armstrong.

Japanese: く‥‥クワ？　何かしら‥‥。
English: Q-Quoi? What do you mean?

Japanese: 被害者は事件当時、イヤホンで ラジオを聞いていました。
English: At the time in question, the victim was listening to the radio with his earpiece.

Japanese: ‥‥《長者ラジオ》‥‥
English: The show he was listening to was "Millionaire Radio".

Japanese: 毎週、５０００万円の当選番号を 番組の中で発表しています。
English: Each week, they announce the winning number of a half million dollar lottery ticket.

Japanese: ムッシュ・タカオは、 その番組をきいていたワケね‥‥
English: Oui. Z'at must be la show Mon. Elg was listening to.

Japanese: まったく、問題ありませんな。
English: I can't see any problem with this testimony, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: そうでしょうか‥‥？
English: I wonder...

Japanese: 被害者たちが来店したとき‥‥ 午後２時をすぎていたそうですね？
English: You say the victim arrived at your restaurant after 2 PM, correct?

Japanese: マチガイないわ！ ハッキリ思い出しちゃったから。
English: Oui, oui. I am sure of it. I remember it perfectly now!

Japanese: ランチのサービス・タイムが 終わる時間なのよねえ。
English: I know it was z'at time, because I 'ad just finished serving la lunch menu.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうコトだ？ まるほどう‥‥
English: Get to the point, Trite. If you have one.

Japanese: この番組は、午後１時３０分から 始まるんですよ。
English: That show is broadcast live at 1:30 PM.

Japanese: ‥‥地上で最もスリリングな １０分間を放送するために‥‥
English: And it claims to be "the most thrilling 10 minutes of your life"...
I-It's on the air at 1:30!?

Japanese: 被害者は、大騒ぎしたそうですね。 “宝クジが当たった”‥‥と。
English: Now, supposedly, the victim made some noise when it was announced that he had won.

Japanese: しかし‥‥そんなことは あり得ない。ゼッタイに！
English: And yet... I don't believe his cry of joy could have occurred after 2 PM!

Japanese: その３０分も前に、番組は とっくに終わっていたのだから！
English: Because the show had already finished more than 30 minutes earlier by that point in time!

Japanese: のーーーーーーん！
English: Nooooooooon!

Japanese: この《被害者》は、 あり得ない行動ばかりとっている！
English: This "victim" we've been told about has done nothing but the impossible!

Japanese: 聞こえるはずのないラジオ、 終わってしまったはずの番組‥‥
English: Listening to the radio with a ruptured eardrum, catching a show that was already over...

Japanese: それらのムジュンを解決する コタエは、１つしかない！
English: There is only one conclusion you can draw from these facts!

Japanese: すべては、本来の犯行時刻から ３０分ズレた、ニセモノだった！
English: This "victim" was an imposter, acting out the poisoning 30 minutes after the real murder!

Japanese: あの日、吐麗美庵には ２人の岡　高夫がいたのです。
English: Yes, there were two Glen Elgs in Trés Bien that day.

Japanese: 真犯人に毒を盛られて死んだ、 ホンモノの岡　高夫と‥‥
English: The real Glen Elg, now dead, having been poisoned by the real killer...

Japanese: 五十嵐　将兵に見せるために 仕組まれた、ニセモノの岡　高夫！
English: ...and the phony Glen Elg, acting out the events for Mr. Kudo to witness!

Japanese: た‥‥たしかに‥‥
English: I-It certainly seems that way...

Japanese: そう考えなければ、時間のズレに 説明がつきません！
English: I mean, if that wasn't the case, how could you explain the time discrepancy?

Japanese: 調子に乗ってるようだな、 まるほどう‥‥。
English: Quite a performance, Trite. You were almost on a roll.

Japanese: だが‥‥アンタの乗ってるのは、 ガキのリズム、エイトビートだぜ！
English: But sadly, you lack the rock hard foundation of rhythm to build your song!

Japanese: （“調子に乗る”って、 そういうコトなのか‥‥？）
English: (...What is this? Music Theory 101?)

Japanese: いいか！　アンタのムチャな 主張どおり‥‥午後２時すぎ。
English: Let's recap. According to your "imaginative" theory, it's now just after 2 PM...

Japanese: ニセモノの岡　高夫が、ジイさん 相手にシバイを見せたのならば！
English: The phony Elg is performing a play for the benefit of Mr. Kudo.

Japanese: そのとき、ホンモノの岡　高夫は どうなっていたんだ！
English: How do you explain then, where the real Glen Elg is!?

Japanese: そ、そんなの‥‥ 考えるまでもありません！
English: I don't believe I have to spell this out for the court! However!

Japanese: ホンモノは、とっくに 毒殺されていたはずです！
English: At that time, the real Glen Elg was already dead!

Japanese: ‥‥当然、そうでしょう。
English: That's certainly the obvious conclusion.

Japanese: ‥‥かかったな、まるほどう‥‥
English: Thank you, Trite. That's exactly what I was hoping you would say.

Japanese: な、なんだと‥‥！
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: それなら‥‥アンタには 立証できる、ってワケだな？
English: Now, I presume you can prove this theory of yours...?

Japanese: その《死体》が、いったい どこに消えちまったのか！
English: Can you explain where the missing corpse went to!?

Japanese: し、死体が‥‥“消えた”‥‥？
English: Th-The missing corpse...?

Japanese: “店内に、客は１人きりだった” それが、ジイさんの証言だ。
English: According to the old man's testimony, there was only one other customer there.

Japanese: その“客”が、ニセモノの 岡　高夫だったとしたら‥‥
English: If that "customer" was the phony Glen Elg...

Japanese: 犯人は、ホンモノの死体を どこかにかくしたハズだぜ！
English: ...then where did the killer hide the body of the real victim!?

Japanese: あぎゃあああああああッ！
English: Naaaaaargh!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。 お聞きのとおりです。
English: The prosecution has a valid point, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: あなたが、自分の仮説を 立証するためには‥‥
English: If your theory is to stand up to examination by the court...

Japanese: 次の問題に、カンペキに 答えてもらう必要があります。
English: ...you must provide us with proof by answering the prosecution's question.

Japanese: すなわち。犯人が、 『死体をどこにかくしたか？』
English: Where did the killer hide the body?

Japanese: は‥‥はい‥‥
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor...

Japanese: もちろん‥‥当てずっぽうは ユルされねえ。
English: No conjecture, Trite. Let's hear some facts for once.

Japanese: 死体がかくされた場所‥‥ 《証拠品》で立証してもらうぜ。
English: Show the court a piece of evidence that proves where the body was hidden!

Japanese: しょ、証拠品‥‥！
English: E-Evidence!?

Japanese: なな、なんか急に 風が冷たくなってきたよ！
English: Wh-What's with the intense pressure in here all of a sudden!?

Japanese: （ムジュンを暴いて 追いつめたつもりが‥‥）
English: (I thought I had him with that contradiction.)

Japanese: （いつの間にか、こっちが 追いつめられている‥‥！）
English: (But he's turned it all around and backed me into a corner instead!)

Japanese: それでは、成歩堂くん！ ‥‥うかがいましょう。
English: Well, Mr. Wright? The court will now hear the defense's theory and evidence.

Japanese: ホンモノの岡　高夫の死体が かくされた場所は、どこですかな？
English: First, where was the body of the real Mr. Elg concealed?

Japanese: 店内に死体をかくす場所は、 どこにもありません。
English: Clearly, there's no place to hide a body inside Trés Bien.

Japanese: 当然‥‥死体は、 店外に持ち出されたはずです！
English: Therefore, the killer must have hauled the body outside!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥なるほど。
English: Hmm... I see.

Japanese: そこまでは、いいさ。
English: Nice supposition.

Japanese: じゃあ、そいつを立証して もらおうか、まるほどう！
English: But the real question is, "Can you back it up?"

Japanese: 死体が店外に運ばれた‥‥ それを立証する証拠はッ！
English: Where's the evidence that proves the body was taken out of the restaurant?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら‥‥
English: Too bad...

Japanese: やはり、ガキのリズムには、 限界があったようだぜ。
English: I don't think you'll ever grasp the real heart of Rock'n'Roll.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうコトですか！
English: What are you talking about?

Japanese: 今の証拠品は、調子はずれ。 ‥‥そういうコトです。
English: He means that your evidence is irrelevant, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ダメだよ！　ちゃんと 《証拠》まで考えなくちゃ！
English: Come on, Nick! You've got to think it through!

Japanese: “ろっけん・ろーる”で 行こうよ、なるほどくん！
English: Feel the beat! And then hit him with some heavy metal!

Japanese: （‥‥どうする！ 落ちついて考えるんだ‥‥）
English: (OK, I need to calm down and get my facts straight. What should I do now?)

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度‥‥ アタマを冷やして考えてみます。
English: I-I think I need to clear my head...

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　オレの冷たい視線で 冷やすがいいさ。
English: Ha...! I'd say it's already empty, Trite!

Japanese: 私も、その視線に協力しましょう。
English: I'm inclined to agree.

Japanese: ミー・トゥー。
English: Yo también.

Japanese: （英語じゃないか、それ！）
English: (What's with the Spanish all of a sudden!?)

Japanese: （ここまでのロジックに、 まちがいはあり得ない！）
English: (There's nothing wrong with my logic so far, I'm sure of it!)

Japanese: （証拠品を‥‥もう一度！ 提示するんだ！）
English: (I'll hit them with another piece of evidence and see if that helps...)

Japanese: 死体を店外に運びだすのは、 キケンが大きすぎます。
English: It'd would have been too dangerous to take the body outside.

Japanese: 当然‥‥店内のどこかに かくしたはずです！
English: Obviously, the body must have been hidden somewhere inside Trés Bien.

Japanese: それは、おもしろい‥‥。
English: Hmm... Interesting...

Japanese: いったい、店内のどこに かくすことができたのか？
English: But where could a body have been hidden inside a restaurant?

Japanese: この上面図で、 示していただきましょう。
English: Perhaps you would care to show the court on these plans, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: 吐麗美庵の店内で‥‥ 死体がかくされていたのはッ！
English: The exact location where the body was concealed inside Trés Bien is...

Japanese: 死体をかくした場所‥‥ それは、ここです！
English: The body was hidden here!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥なるほど。
English: Hmm... I see.

Japanese: そこまでは、いいさ。
English: Nice supposition.

Japanese: じゃあ、そいつを立証して もらおうか、まるほどう！
English: But the real question is, "Can you back it up?"

Japanese: 死体が、そこにかくされた‥‥ それを立証する証拠はッ！
English: Where's the evidence that proves the body was hidden in that location!?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら‥‥
English: Too bad...

Japanese: やはり、ガキのリズムには、 限界があったようだぜ。
English: I don't think you'll ever grasp the real heart of Rock'n'Roll.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうコトですか！
English: What are you talking about?

Japanese: 今の証拠品は、調子はずれ。 ‥‥そういうコトです。
English: He means that your evidence is irrelevant, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ダメだよ！　ちゃんと 《証拠》まで考えなくちゃ！
English: Come on, Nick! You've got to think it through!

Japanese: “ろっけん・ろーる”で 行こうよ、なるほどくん！
English: Feel the beat! And then hit him with some heavy metal!

Japanese: （‥‥どうする！ 落ちついて考えるんだ‥‥）
English: (OK, I need to calm down and get my facts straight. What should I do now?)

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度‥‥ アタマを冷やして考えてみます。
English: I-I think I need to clear my head...

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　オレの冷たい視線で 冷やすがいいさ。
English: Ha...! I'd say it's already empty, Trite!

Japanese: 私も、その視線に協力しましょう。
English: I'm inclined to agree.

Japanese: ミー・トゥー。
English: Yo también.

Japanese: （英語じゃないか、それ！）
English: (What's with the Spanish all of a sudden!?)

Japanese: （ここまでのロジックに、 まちがいはあり得ない！）
English: (There's nothing wrong with my logic so far, I'm sure of it!)

Japanese: （もう一度！　死体をかくせる 場所を提示するんだ！）
English: (I'll have another go at proving where the body was hidden.)

Japanese: 本土坊さん。 この小ビンに、見おぼえは？
English: Mr. Armstrong! Do you recognize this bottle?

Japanese: の、ノンノン！　そんな こきたないビン、知らないわ。
English: Non! Non, non, non! I 'ave never seen z'at ugly bottle before in my life!

Japanese: ワタシが愛用しているのは、コレ。 モルニーフル社の最高級品よぉ。
English: I only use la very best bottles, monsieur! Z'e 'ighest quality only for me!

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。その小ビン‥‥、 どこで見つけたのですかな？
English: Where was that bottle found, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: フシギなコトに‥‥ 吐麗美庵のキッチンなんです。
English: Interestingly enough, Your Honor, it was found in the kitchen of Trés Bien.

Japanese: コム！　なんですって‥‥
English: Eh? Quoi!?

Japanese: でも。アロマ・オイルのボトルは これしか使わないコトに‥‥
English: But I only ever use z'ese bottles for my aromatherapy oils.

Japanese: ザンネンながら‥‥コイツの中身は アロマ・オイルではありません。
English: But this bottle doesn't contain aromatherapy oil, Mr. Armstrong.

Japanese: ‥‥外用薬です。
English: No, it contains a medication.

Japanese: く‥‥クスリ‥‥
English: What kind of medication?

Japanese: もちろん、おぼえていますね？
English: I'm sure everyone remembers, don't they?

Japanese: 岡　高夫さんが事件当日、 耳鼻科へ行っていたことを！
English: That Mr. Elg visited an otolaryngological clinic, and was given medication that day!

Japanese: ま‥‥まさか‥‥
English: Y-You can't be serious...

Japanese: ブンセキの結果は、 すでに出ています！
English: The defense had the contents of the bottle analyzed, and I have the lab results here!

Japanese: これは、そのときに 処方されたクスリなのです！
English: The contents of the bottle match the prescription that was given to Mr. Elg!

Japanese: ぶへえええッ！
English: Bwooooar!

Japanese: 岡　高夫さんを毒殺した犯人は、 死体をキッチンにかくした！
English: Glen Elg's murderer hid the body in the restaurant kitchen.

Japanese: そのとき‥‥被害者のポケットから コイツが落ちたのです。
English: At which time, this bottle fell out of the victim's pocket.

Japanese: 本土坊さん！ ‥‥事件が起こったとき‥‥
English: Mr. Armstrong!

Japanese: あなたは、キッチンに いたそうですね‥‥？
English: When the incident occurred, didn't you say you were in the kitchen?

Japanese: ももも、もん・でゅ‥‥
English: M-M-Mon dieu!

Japanese: そう‥‥死体をかくしたのは あなただった！
English: Yes, you know what I'm about to say. It was YOU who hid the victim's body!

Japanese: あなたは、被告人・須々木　マコを 守るフリをして‥‥
English: You did a fine job pretending to defend my client, Maggey Byrde. However!

Japanese: そのウラで、彼女を犯人に 仕立て上げようとしたのです！
English: You were setting her up to take the fall behind the poor girl's back!

Japanese: のーーーーーーーーーーーーーん！
English: Nooooooooon!

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！　静粛に！ おどろくべき事態ですッ！
English: O-Order! Order! This is an extraordinary development.

Japanese: 証人！　あなたが‥‥ 岡　高夫さんを殺害したのですか！
English: Witness! Did you... Did you murder Mr. Glen Elg?

Japanese: ち、ちがうの！ ワタシ、そんなコト‥‥
English: Never! I could not do such an 'orrible thing!

Japanese: うおおおおおおおおおおおおおおお おおおおおおおおおおおおおおおお
English: Nooooooooooooooooooooooooo oooooooooooooooooooooooooo

Japanese: おおおおおおおおおおおおおおおお おおおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: oooooooooooooooooooooooooo ooooooooooooooooooooooooo!

Japanese: ごごご、ゴドー検事‥‥？
English: M-Mr. Godot!?
...The bitterness...

Japanese: ウソを１つ聞かされるたび、カップ のコーヒーを１杯、飲みほす‥‥
English: Every time I get lied to, I always down a mug of coffee.

Japanese: ‥‥そいつが、オレのルールだぜ。
English: ...That's one of my rules.

Japanese: （何杯目だと思ってるんだよ！）
English: (Do you have the slightest idea how many cups you've had by now...?)

Japanese: そして‥‥そのウソをついた クソ野郎に‥‥
English: Then I like to do the same to the person who lied to me.

Japanese: カラになったコーヒーのカップを １ケ、飲みこませる‥‥
English: I like to take them down with my empty cup!

Japanese: そこのコックさんよォ‥‥。
English: Listen up, chef!

Japanese: アンタも１ケ、 飲みこんでみるかい！
English: How about a brand new flavor in your ear, my "h" deficient friend?

Japanese: ジュ・ヴ・デュマン・パルドン！ 聞いてちょうだいッ！
English: Je vous demande pardon! Please, you must 'ear me out!

Japanese: これはワナなのよ！ 聞いてったら！　‥‥プリーズ‥‥
English: It is a trap! Listen to me! Por favor!

Japanese: “プリーズ”は英語です。
English: Yo hablo Español, Mr. Armstrong, and "Por favor" is Spanish.

Japanese: 一度だけ聞くぜ。 ‥‥やったのかい？　アンタが‥‥
English: I'm only going to ask you once. Did you do it?

Japanese: の‥‥ノンノンノンノン！ 断じて‥‥極めてノンよッ！
English: Non! Non, non! Absolutely non!

Japanese: わ‥‥ワタシは、ただ‥‥ I...
English: I simply...

Japanese: ‥‥いいだろうぜ。
English: Let's hear it.

Japanese: 最後に一度だけ、くれてやるさ。 ‥‥なあ、ジイさん。
English: You've got one shot. Right, gramps?

Japanese: 証人に‥‥ 発言のチャンスを与えましょう。
English: Witness! The court will permit you the chance to make one final statement.

Japanese: もし、またウソをついた場合。 ゴドー検事のコーヒーカップと‥‥
English: If you lie under oath again, Mr. Godot's coffee mug awaits you...

Japanese: ‥‥私の、この木槌を 飲みこんでいただきます。
English: As does my gavel!

Japanese: ‥‥ウイ。 わかりました‥‥。
English: O-Oui... It is clear.

Japanese: （‥‥なんなんだ、 このミョーなフンイキは‥‥）
English: (What do they always say in the movies... "I've got a bad feeling about this...")

Japanese: ‥‥それでは！ 最後の証言をおねがいします！
English: Very well! Begin your final testimony, Mr. Armstrong!

Japanese: ～自　供～
English: -- The Confession --

Japanese: ワタシ‥‥たしかに、死体を キッチンにかくしたわ。
English: It is true, I 'id la body in la kitchen.

Japanese: あるオトコに命令されて‥‥ しかたがなかったの。
English: A man forced me to do it. I 'ad no choice.

Japanese: ‥‥協力するしかなかった。 断れない理由があったから‥‥
English: I 'ad to go along w'iz 'im because... Z'ere was a reason why I could not refuse.

Japanese: でも！　ワタシ、殺人なんて やってないの！　信じて‥‥！
English: But I did not kill 'im! I swear it! You must believe me!

Japanese: “協力”‥‥？ いったい、だれに！
English: You were "forced"? By who!?

Japanese: い、言えないわ！ ワタシ‥‥消されちゃう！
English: I-I cannot say! Or I will be... erased!

Japanese: じゃあ、他のことを聞きましょう。 岡　高夫が死んだとき‥‥
English: Let's try a different question then.

Japanese: ‥‥テーブルには、本当に 彼しかいなかったのですか？
English: When Mr. Elg died, was he really the only person at his table?

Japanese: ‥‥いたわ。
English: Z'ere was...

Japanese: もうヒトリ‥‥オトコが。
English: Z'ere was another man.

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥）
English: (I knew it!)

Japanese: マコちゃん‥‥ホントのコト、 話してくれていたんだね‥‥
English: Maggey was telling the truth!

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。‥‥尋問を。
English: You may cross-examine the witness now, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （もう‥‥ぼくのするべきコトは １つしか残っていない）
English: (There's just one more thing I need to do.)

Japanese: （彼がかくしている“真犯人” その名前を暴くだけだ‥‥）
English: (I gotta break this guy, and get him to tell us the name of the real killer.)

Japanese: ～自　供～
English: -- The Confession --

Japanese: ワタシ‥‥たしかに、死体を キッチンにかくしたわ。
English: It is true, I 'id la body in la kitchen.

Japanese: あるオトコに命令されて‥‥ しかたがなかったの。
English: A man forced me to do it. I 'ad no choice.

Japanese: ‥‥協力するしかなかった。 断れない理由があったから‥‥
English: I 'ad to go along w'iz 'im because... Z'ere was a reason why I could not refuse.

Japanese: でも！　ワタシ、殺人なんて やってないの！　信じて‥‥！
English: But I did not kill 'im! I swear it! You must believe me!

Japanese: ここまで来たら、もう まわりクドいことはやめようよ！
English: He's already confessed this much! He might as well stop dancing around the real issue!

Japanese: 本土坊さん、かくし通すつもり らしいな。真犯人の名前‥‥
English: Yeah. But he really doesn't want to tell us who the killer is.

Japanese: バシッとたたきつけちゃおうよ。 ミもフタもなく。
English: Then sock it to him, Nick!

Japanese: （それがいいみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Yeah, that's not a bad idea!)

Japanese: あなたが運んだんですか？ 岡　高夫さんを‥‥
English: Did you carry the body by yourself?

Japanese: ウイ。彼と‥‥マコちゃんを。
English: Oui. I carried 'im. And I carried Maggey, too.

Japanese: マコちゃん‥‥！
English: Maggey, too!?

Japanese: 彼女、目の前で被害者が 倒れたとき、キゼツしちゃって。
English: When she saw la victim collapse, she fainted.

Japanese: ‥‥ほっておくワケに いかなかったから‥‥。
English: I could not leave 'er z'ere.

Japanese: どうして、そんなコトを‥‥？
English: But why did you hide the bodies?

Japanese: その“オトコ”‥‥ だれなんですか？
English: What man? Who was he?

Japanese: ダメ！　言えないわ！ お、恐ろしくて‥‥
English: Non! Non, I cannot say! I fear for my life!

Japanese: （そうとうキツく オドされてるみたいだな‥‥）
English: (He's really scared...)

Japanese: なるほどくん、かわりに 言ってあげるしかないね。
English: You'll just have to put the words in his mouth, Nick.

Japanese: ‥‥そうだな。 つきつけてやるか‥‥。
English: Yeah, you're right. If he won't tell me, I'll tell him.

Japanese: しかし‥‥なぜ、 そのオトコの言いなりに？
English: But why would you go along with this man?

Japanese: その“理由”というのは‥‥？
English: And what reason would that be, Mr. Armstrong?

Japanese: ‥‥知ってるクセに‥‥
English: You know, monsieur...
Yes?

Japanese: そんな恥ずかしいコト、 オトメのクチから言わせるつもり？
English: Surely you cannot expect a young maiden to talk about such an embarrassment?

Japanese: （“オトメ”っていうトシかよ！）
English: (A "maiden"!? You're a bit old to get away with that!)

Japanese: トシの問題じゃないと思うけど‥‥
English: And a bit too male!

Japanese: とにかく、尋問を終わらせるには、 ぼくが立証するしかないね。
English: I can't finish the cross-examination without establishing his reason.

Japanese: ‥‥《証拠品》で！
English: So I'll just have to prove it... with evidence!

Japanese: “死体をかくしただけ” ‥‥ですか？
English: So, you are claiming that all you did was hide the bodies? Is that correct?

Japanese: そ、そうなの！
English: Oui! Z'at's right!

Japanese: それならば‥‥ 証言してもらいたいですな。
English: If we are to believe you, Mr. Armstrong, you must tell the court everything!

Japanese: あなたが協力した “オトコ”の名前を。
English: You must make clear the identity of the man who ordered you to do this.

Japanese: もう終わりにしましょう、 本土坊さん。
English: Let's end this dance of Ring Around the Rosie, Mr. Armstrong.

Japanese: あなたが協力したのは‥‥ このオトコですね。
English: This is the man that you've been referring to!

Japanese: あうううう‥‥
English: Aaaah!

Japanese: ど、どなたですかッ！
English: Who is that!?

Japanese: なんか、どこかで 見たコトがあるような‥‥
English: I feel like I've seen him somewhere before...

Japanese: （‥‥１ヶ月前の裁判で、 法廷に立っていたからな‥‥）
English: (Oh, I don't know... Maybe a month ago in this very courtroom?)

Japanese: ‥‥芝九蔵　虎ノ助。闇金融 《カリヨーゼ》の社長です。
English: This man is Furio Tigre. He's the manager of a loan office called "Tender Lender".

Japanese: ‥‥もう、かくしてもムダです。 わかっているでしょう‥‥
English: There's no point trying to hide the truth anymore, Mr. Armstrong.

Japanese: あなたは、彼には逆らえなかった。 ‥‥コイツがあるかぎり！
English: I know you couldn't go against Mr. Tigre. At least, not while he had this on you!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。今日はもう、 帰ったほうがいいですな。
English: I can tell today has worn you out. You have my permission to go home, Mr. Wright.
E-Excuse me?

Japanese: きっと、つかれてるのねえ。
English: Oui. You are tired.

Japanese: この、マートルとラベンダーの オイルを軽くブレンドして‥‥
English: Z'is oil is a mild blend of myrtle and lavender...

Japanese: シンパイいらねえさ。
English: Don't worry, Trite.

Japanese: あとは、オレたちで うまくやっておくぜ。
English: We've got this covered.

Japanese: （うまくやられてタマるか！）
English: (Covered!? Like I'm going to leave it to you to finish!)

Japanese: 本土坊さん！　ごまかすのは もうやめてください。
English: Mr. Armstrong! No more games!

Japanese: あなたには、５０００万円の 借金がありますね。
English: You have a half a million dollar debt, don't you?

Japanese: ご、ごせんまんえん！ そうなのですか、証人！
English: H-Half a million dollars!? Is this true, Mr. Armstrong?

Japanese: ‥‥‥ジュ・スイ・デゾレ‥‥ 借りちゃいました。
English: Oui! Je suis désolé! I was weak, and I borrowed la money!

Japanese: これが、本土坊さんの 致命的な弱点だったのです。
English: This is Mr. Armstrong's Achilles heel.

Japanese: だから‥‥この人物に逆らうことは ゼッタイにできなかった！
English: And that's why you couldn't refuse anything asked of you by this man!

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。今日はもう、 帰ったほうがいいですな。
English: I can tell today has worn you out. You have my permission to go home, Mr. Wright.
E-Excuse me?

Japanese: きっと、つかれてるのねえ。
English: Oui. You are tired.

Japanese: この、マートルとラベンダーの オイルを軽くブレンドして‥‥
English: Z'is oil is a mild blend of myrtle and lavender...

Japanese: シンパイいらねえさ。
English: Don't worry, Trite.

Japanese: あとは、オレたちで うまくやっておくぜ。
English: We've got this covered.

Japanese: （うまくやられてタマるか！）
English: (Covered!? Like I'm going to leave it to you to finish!)

Japanese: とにかく、本土坊さんには、 致命的な弱点がありました。
English: Anyway, the point I am trying to make is that Mr. Armstrong has this one, fatal weakness.

Japanese: ‥‥闇金融からの、 ５０００万円の借金。
English: A half million dollar loan from a black-market loan shark.

Japanese: あなたには、返済の アテはまったくなかった。
English: And you had no way of paying it back, did you?

Japanese: だから‥‥この人物の言うことを 聞かざるを得なかった‥‥
English: That's why you were forced to do anything this man told you.

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおりよ。
English: Oui, it is as you say.

Japanese: 本土坊さん‥‥
English: Mr. Armstrong...

Japanese: ゼニトラさん‥‥ウチの店を 《打ち合わせに使う》って‥‥。
English: La Tiger... 'E told me 'e was going to use my restaurant for a business rendez-vous.

Japanese: あの日、被害者と待ち合わせて 返済のハナシをしていたの。
English: On la day in question, 'e was meeting la victim to demand z'at 'e repay 'is loan.

Japanese: ‥‥どうして、あんなコトに なったのかは知らないわ。
English: I don't know why it 'appened like zat!

Japanese: ワタシ、あのオトコの言うとおりに 動くしかなかったの。
English: I just did what 'e told me to do. I 'ad no choice!

Japanese: 死体と‥‥キゼツしたマコちゃんを 店内から運び出して‥‥
English: I carried la body and la inconscient Maggey out of la dining area...

Japanese: キッチンにかくしたの。 すべてを見なかったことにして‥‥
English: ...and into z'e kitchen. After z'at, I just tried to forget what I 'ad seen!

Japanese: “あのオトコ”‥‥ 芝九蔵　虎ノ助、ですね。
English: I think we can now safely say that the man who forced your hand was Mr. Furio Tigre.

Japanese: ‥‥証人。 もう１つ、うかがいましょう。
English: Hmm... I do have one further question for you, Mr. Armstrong.

Japanese: ‥‥被告人のエプロンから、 毒薬と宝クジが見つかりましたが、
English: The poison and the lottery ticket were recovered from the defendant's apron pocket.

Japanese: これも、あなたが‥‥？
English: Was that your doing as well?

Japanese: ち‥‥ちがう！ ワタシは知らなかった！
English: Non! I knew nothing about z'at!

Japanese: マコちゃんにツミを着せる なんて‥‥ワタシ‥‥
English: Making it look like it was Maggey who 'ad done it... I was... I was not...

Japanese: 聞かされてなかったわ！ そんな、ヒドいコト‥‥
English: It is déspicable!

Japanese: ゴドー検事！
English: Mr. Godot!

Japanese: ‥‥芝九蔵　虎ノ助を 証人として召喚してください。
English: You will summon this Furio Tigre as a witness!

Japanese: 本日中にはムリでしょうから、 審理は中断します。
English: I doubt that can be arranged today, so we will adjourn for now.

Japanese: このつづきは、明日‥‥
English: Proceedings will continue tomorrow...
...Thirty minutes.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですか？
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: オレは、審理をつづけるぜ。
English: The trial will go on. I'll see to it myself.

Japanese: ３０分後‥‥コイツを 証言台に立たせる。‥‥かならず。
English: I need half an hour to get that guy on the stand. Not a minute more.

Japanese: ご、ゴドー検事‥‥
English: H-How the...?

Japanese: 安心するのは早いぜ、 まるほどう‥‥
English: Don't sit back and relax yet, Trite!

Japanese: そこのコックが、本当のコトを 言ってるかどうか‥‥？
English: No one knows if that chef is really telling the truth or not.

Japanese: まだまだ、判決の行方は わからねえさ。
English: This trial could still go either way.

Japanese: わかりました。 あなたにまかせましょう。
English: Very well. Your request is granted, Mr. Godot.

Japanese: 芝九蔵　虎ノ助の到着しだい、 審理を再開します。
English: We will resume once Mr. Tigre is ready to take the stand.

Japanese: それでは‥‥
English: Until then...

Japanese: これより、３０分間の 休憩に入ります！
English: Court is adjourned for a 30 minute recess!

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、須々木　マコに たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Maggey Byrde...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately...

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: ...to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. This court is adjourned!
